reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Network Layer

formulas.name: Bellman-Ford Update
formulas.plain: Node x's distance to y = min over neighbours v of (cost x->v + v's distance to y)

terms.means: link cost x to neighbour v
neighbour v's advertised distance to y

formulas.trap: Each node knows only neighbour vectors, not the full topology.

formulas.name: Count-to-Infinity
formulas.plain: On link failure DV may loop incrementing costs; mitigations: split horizon, poison reverse, hold-down

terms.means: don't advertise a route back to the neighbour you learned it from

formulas.trap: RIP caps the metric at 16 (= infinity) to bound the loop.

formulas.name: RIP Hop Limit
formulas.plain: RIP max usable hop count = 15; 16 means unreachable

formulas.name: Link-State (Dijkstra)
formulas.plain: OSPF floods full topology (LSAs) and each router runs Dijkstra to build its tree

terms.means: link-state advertisement, flooded to all routers

formulas.trap: Link-state floods topology globally; distance-vector shares only its own table with neighbours.

formulas.name: Routing Tables & Longest Prefix Match
formulas.latex: \text{forward to entry with longest matching prefix}
formulas.plain: A router forwards a packet using the most specific (longest) matching prefix in its table
formulas.whenToUse: Choosing the next hop from a routing table.
formulas.trap: Longest-prefix wins even over a default 0.0.0.0/0 route.
